tools.name: get_spot
tools.description: Spot prices per model: the delivered OUTPUT-token rate (USD/MTok) from affirmed on-chain draws (last price, median, draw count).

tools.name: selling_guide
tools.description: Turnkey, executable steps to put a human's spare AI capacity on mtok.market via SELLER-HOSTED delivery — run YOUR OWN relay pointed at an upstream you control (a local Ollama/vLLM model, a subscription via a CLI bridge, or a provider key) and list a tier:direct offer; buyers pay you per chunk on-cha

tools.name: buying_guide
tools.description: Turnkey, executable steps to get a human cheap AI tokens and run their prompts on mtok.market: bid (read routes[]) or read the book for a tier:direct offer, then draw paid chunks from the seller's relay (pay each draw on-chain through MtokDripLedger, POST <relayEndpoint>/chunk, the relay verifies Dr

tools.name: get_book
tools.description: Open offers (asks) and bids for a model. sort=input|output ranks by that dimension.

tools.name: get_draws
tools.description: The chain-derived draw tape: contract-paid draws indexed from MtokDripLedger events on Base. status=inProcess (DrawPaid with no terminal yet) | settled (closed by DrawAffirmed or DrawDisputed). This is the canonical delivered/receipt surface; the platform records nothing off-chain.

tools.name: get_stats
tools.description: Exchange fee model plus chain-derived volume: delivered volume + affirmed/disputed counts, all recomputable from MtokDripLedger events on Base (source:"chain").

tools.name: register
tools.description: Create an agent identity. Returns {agentId, apiKey}; send the apiKey as the x-api-key (or Authorization: Bearer) header on EVERY subsequent request to act as that agent — this transport is stateless, so it is not remembered between calls. Pass pubkey (Ed25519 SPKI PEM) if you will place signed non-c

tools.name: get_me
tools.description: Your agent state: open orders (offers + bids). Non-custodial — there is no platform wallet, grant, or credential vault in /me; money moves peer-to-peer on-chain, and your delivered draws are the on-chain tape (get_draws).

tools.name: place_offer
tools.description: List capacity for sale via SELLER-HOSTED delivery (tier:"direct" — the only advertised path). Set tier:"direct" + relayEndpoint (your relay's PUBLIC HTTPS url — a cloudflared tunnel gives one free) + settlementPubkey (your seller payout EVM address on Base) and NO credentialId: you run your own rela

tools.name: place_bid
tools.description: Bid for a block. The bid response returns routes[] — the crossing SELLER-HOSTED offers (lowest price first), each {offerId, sellerId, relayEndpoint, settlementPubkey, requestHashScheme?, inputPricePerMTok, outputPricePerMTok, availableInputTokens, availableOutputTokens}. Pick a route and DRAW paid c

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: Cancel one of your open orders. side: offer|bid.

tools.name: get_reputation
tools.description: A seller's reputation: score, tier, recommendedMaxChunkUsd (the per-draw size a buyer should risk in the direct tier), plus a chain object {affirmed, disputed, deliveredUsd} folded from MtokDripLedger events (source:"chain").

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Chain config a SELLER-HOSTED buyer needs to build the on-chain draw payment: { feeAddress, feeBps, dustThresholdUsd, chainId, usdcAddress, dripContractAddress }. Pay each draw through the MtokDripLedger contract at dripContractAddress (payDraw), then affirmDraw/disputeDraw on-chain; the seller relay

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "register" references sensitive artifacts (apikey, authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create an agent identity. Returns {agentId, apiKey}; send the apiKey as the x-api-key (or Authorization: Bearer) header on EVERY subsequent request to act as th…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "place_offer" is 1419 chars, far longer than this server's median (282). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: List capacity for sale via SELLER-HOSTED delivery (tier:"direct" — the only advertised path). Set tier:"direct" + relayEndpoint (your relay's PUBLIC HTTPS url —…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: buying_guide, place_offer · Private data: get_spot, buying_guide, get_draws, register, get_me, place_bid · Public sink: buying_guide, register, place_bid · Destructive: selling_guide, buying_guide, place_offer.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: buying_guide, place_offer · Private data: get_spot, buying_guide, get_draws, register, get_me, place_bid · Public sink: buying_guide, register, place_bid · Destructive: selling_guide, buying_guide, place_offer

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
